title: 6 Managing Dishonoured PADS, Deposit Errors, and Chargebacks for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
body_text:
6	Managing Dishonoured PADS, Deposit Errors, and Chargebacks for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
6.1	Managing Dishonoured Pre-Authorized Debits (PADs) for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
Central 1 creates PADs (the sweep) to debit Receiver General Concentrator holding accounts and credit the Receiver Generalâs Concentrator account held at Central 1. If a Receiver General Concentrator holding account PAD is dishonoured, contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialists immediately. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details. PADs must not be returned through the clearing.
6.2	Managing Deposit Errors for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
If you discover an error (e.g., a PAD was processed for the wrong amount, or duplicate Receiver General Deposit requests), you must submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow. Central 1 will investigate the deposit. If Central 1 determines that an adjustment is required, an AFT transaction is initiated to the appropriate Receiver General Concentrator holding account. If applicable, Central 1 must wait until sufficient funds are available in the Central 1 Receiver General Concentrator Account held at Central 1 to debit the Receiver General to recover funds. The adjustment appears on the Automated Transfer System Detailed Listing (CCD) report within two business days of processing the adjustment. Financial institutions can access adjustment notices on the Online Return System (ORS). See the Online Return System (ORS) Part for more information.
Note: 	You canât charge overdraft fees to the Receiver General Concentrator holding account if an error caused a negative account balance. Your financial institution is credited $1.50 per Receiver General deposit at month-end. This credit covers any potential fees that the account may incur.

Your financial institution is responsible for clerical errors made during deposits to the Receiver General Concentrator holding account and when submitting a Receiver General Deposit Request in ServiceNow. You are also responsible for monitoring a Receiver General Concentrator holding account to ensure itâs at a zero balance each day and that all adjustments are processed. If an adjustment wasnât posted to the account, request an update on the open ServiceNow ticket or contact Central 1 Payment Services Specialists to determine if an adjustment is pending. See Section 1.2, Contacts and Websites, for contact details.
6.3	Managing Chargebacks for a Receiver General Concentrator Holding Account
Cheques included in Receiver General Concentrator holding account deposits must be returned to Central 1 for processing if dishonoured. To ensure dishonoured items are returned to Central 1 and not to your financial institution, you must enter the Central 1 Receiver General Account transit number (71620-809) in the return information fields when processing Receiver General Concentrator holding account cheques in Branch Capture. See the Deposit Processing Part for procedures on entering return information.
If you receive a misdirected chargeback for the Receiver General Deposit, submit a Paper Payments and Cash Returns Trace Request in ServiceNow to redirect the chargeback to Central 1. After processing, Central 1 sends you a chargeback voucher and copy of the returned item by email, or via your ServiceNow ticket (for misdirected chargebacks). Provide the returned item to the appropriate federal department or agency to recover the funds from their customer.
Figure 6: Sample Chargeback Voucher
